Wirthlin, in Conference Report, Apr. 2001, 95; or Ensign, May 2001, 74)

Alma's interview with Helaman is a classic "wet" and refreshing performance. It is a short, three-question, forty-five-second exchange between father and son. According to the record, Alma was approaching the end of his ministry. He knew that he must select someone to assume prophetic and record-keeping responsibilities. Helaman was his choice. Therefore, Alma came to his son and asked: "Believest thou the words which I spake unto thee concerning those records which have been kept?" Without…

It wasn't bread the people needed most, it was words-the words of God. Mini Lesson: The word of God is not as powerful but more powerful than the sword…
